title: Establish a scoped Ruff formatting baseline for RepoBrief navigation
goal: Resolve the pinned Ruff formatter drift in the RepoBrief call-navigation files through a reviewable style-only change and make the applicable formatting gate explicit without mixing semantic refactors into the cleanup.

acceptance.assertion: The exact repository-pinned Ruff version formats and then accepts the four T029 navigation and benchmark files with no remaining formatter drift.
AST-normalized Python structure and all focused, integration, parity and complete non-browser test results prove that the cleanup changes formatting only.
The patch is isolated from producer refactors and feature work, explicitly reports broad legacy-file churn and is diff-reviewed as a style-only change before merge.
The repository documents or enforces the intended Ruff format check scope so future tasks cannot report it as passed when only Ruff lint was executed.

metadata.source_finding: ruff format --check with pinned Ruff 0.15.13 reports four T029 files as requiring formatting; repobrief_access.py would produce broad pre-existing style churn.

bureau_always_open_scope.reason: Keep Bureau intake and unrelated registry writes available while the isolated Lenskit formatting cleanup is reviewed.
